_meta.title: F1-TC c4_fused_kernels import integration — silent-exit avoidance plan
_meta.author: reconnaissance-agent (opus-4.6-1m, 1M ctx)
_meta.purpose: Map import-chain landscape before adding `use "c4_fused_kernels"` to train_clm_gpu.hexa; propose three avoidance options + threshold measurement test.

training_file_build_status.interpretation: Current 6-use + 7-unique-module graph DOES NOT trigger silent-exit — build verified via test_scale_7b.hexa 40/40

deltas.unique_modules: 7 → 8 (+1: c4_fused_kernels itself — cuda_ffi/cuda_rtc already in graph)
deltas.max_chain_depth: 2 (unchanged — c4_fused_kernels is depth-1 with depth-2 deps identical to existing)

silent_exit_threshold_analysis.memory_feedback_rule: feedback_hexa_silent_exit_5_imports — chains of 3+ modules can emit empty main
observed_in_memory: Single `use "llm_claude_adapter"` alone triggers it on real modules (not synthetic)
silent_exit_threshold_analysis.real_trigger_mechanism_per_memory: specific syntax patterns in real modules (struct-with-array, deep extern @link, comptime const, comment density)
silent_exit_threshold_analysis.current_6_use_training_file_verdict: DOES NOT TRIGGER — 40/40 regression passes, composed.c size is 291KB for 1.5B build → body emitted, main() populated

rationale_low_risk: c4_fused_kernels.hexa adds ZERO new transitive modules (both cuda_ffi and cuda_rtc already in graph)
c4_fused_kernels.hexa does NOT use @link externs for libraries not already used by cuda_ffi/cuda_rtc
max chain depth stays at 2 — silent-exit-prone cases in memory involved 3+ chains with llm/claude adapters
rationale_non_zero_risk: c4_fused_kernels.hexa has 580 LoC of which ~200 are large NVRTC kernel string literals with escape sequences — parser stress vectors
Has `@convergence(state="ossified", ...)` annotations (comptime metadata) — comptime const path is listed in memory as possible trigger
Comment density is ~40% (design doc inlined) — listed as possible trigger in memory
7th_use_risk_class.verdict_recommendation: DO EMPIRICAL MEASUREMENT before relying on `use "c4_fused_kernels"` — option_c in this plan. If passes, option_a in plan becomes trivial `use` addition. If fails, fall back to option_a (inline) or option_b (composite wrapper).

option_a_direct_use_add.risk: MEDIUM — 7th `use` at chain depth 2; see silent_exit_threshold_analysis. Must verify post-edit with `hexa build training/train_clm_gpu.hexa` + inspect `build/artifacts/train_clm_gpu.c` size > 250KB and `main()` body non-empty
mitigation: Verify `wc -l /tmp/train_clm_gpu.c` > 5000 lines (current baseline is composed.c 291KB ≈ 7000 lines)
grep for `int main` body non-empty via `sed -n '/int main/,/^}/p' /tmp/train_clm_gpu.c | wc -l` > 50
option_a_direct_use_add.recommended_if: Empirical test confirms no silent-exit. Highest ergonomics, lowest LoC

option_b_inline_c4_kernels_into_train_cl.description: Paste c4_fused_kernels.hexa content directly into train_clm_gpu.hexa — no new `use` line needed
option_b_inline_c4_kernels_into_train_cl.where_to_inline: After line 1143 `[train_clm_gpu] kernels: NVRTC compile OK ...` println — as a new §F1-TC section
option_b_inline_c4_kernels_into_train_cl.risk: LOW for silent-exit (no import delta), MEDIUM for maintainability (c4_fused_kernels.hexa is an independent interface; inlining creates drift risk if c4_fused_kernels gets updated separately)
concerns: Global name collisions: c4_fused_kernels defines G_C4_MODULE, G_C4_qkv_proj_fused_fwd, G_C4_qkv_proj_fused_tc_fwd, G_C4_INITIALISED, G_C4_TC_MODE, K_C4_QKV_PROJ_FUSED_FWD, K_C4_QKV_PROJ_FUSED_TC_FWD, K_C4_COMBINED_TU, C4_WMMA_M/N/K, C4_WARP, C4_SM90_SHMEM_MAX, C4_ERR_C2_NOT_LANDED — 14+ names
Function collisions: c4_init_modules, c4_set_tc_mode, c4_is_module_ready, c4_impl_qkv_proj_fwd, plus _c4_arg_ptr/i32/free_args/ceil_div helpers — 8+ names
Inline adds 580 LoC → train_clm_gpu.hexa grows to 2218 LoC. Keeps 6-use baseline but bloats entry file
Violates the C4 pre-claim design intent (c4_fused_kernels_interface.hexa explicit interface/impl split)
option_b_inline_c4_kernels_into_train_cl.recommended_if: Option A empirically triggers silent-exit AND option C cannot replicate the c4 surface without adding other modules

option_c_composite_cuda_kernels_wrapper.description: Fold c4_fused_kernels content into cuda_kernels.hexa (which train_clm_gpu already `use`s) — NO net new `use` in train_clm_gpu.hexa
option_c_composite_cuda_kernels_wrapper.where_to_merge: cuda_kernels.hexa end (append a §C4 FUSED MEGA-KERNELS section)
option_c_composite_cuda_kernels_wrapper.risk: LOW for silent-exit (import count unchanged); LOW for maintainability (cuda_kernels.hexa is already the 'all CUDA kernel' aggregator)
concerns: cuda_kernels.hexa currently only depends on cuda_ffi + cuda_rtc — identical to c4_fused_kernels.hexa deps. Merge-safe.
c4 globals/fns coexist with cuda_kernels globals/fns — must verify no name collisions (G_C4_* prefix keeps them disjoint from G_K_* in cuda_kernels)

Loses the `c4_*` independent init path (c4_init_modules vs gpu_init_kernels) — c4 was deliberately made independent per c4_fused_kernels.hexa:37-38 design (small-model runs pay zero NVRTC cost). Merging breaks this separation — small runs that don't call c4_init_modules still pay nothing at runtime, but cuda_kernels.hexa now carries the c4 globals at rest.

A lighter variant: add `pub use "c4_fused_kernels"` RE-EXPORT from cuda_kernels.hexa (if hexa supports pub use). This would keep the source file split + aggregate the `use` edge at cuda_kernels.hexa. NOTE: UNCERTAIN hexa supports `pub use` — must verify via `grep -r 'pub use' hexa-lang/self/`.
option_c_composite_cuda_kernels_wrapper.recommended_if: Option A fails AND we want to preserve c4_fused_kernels.hexa as a standalone file without inlining

option_d_empirical_threshold_probe.description: Before touching production code, build a dummy probe file to empirically measure where the silent-exit threshold lies with current stage0
files_to_create: training/test_import_chain_probe.hexa (dummy main + chain of 5, 6, 7, 8 use statements to existing training files)
option_d_empirical_threshold_probe.outcome_interpretation: If the probe at 7-use emits a full main(), option_a is safe. If it emits empty main(), option_b/c required
option_d_empirical_threshold_probe.recommended_if: We want empirical ground truth before committing to any option — also updates memory:feedback_hexa_silent_exit_5_imports with real threshold

recommendation.recommended: option_a (direct `use` add) + option_d (empirical probe in parallel on Mac). If the existing 40/40 regression passes post-edit AND composed.c size > 250KB, declare victory. If either fails, fall back to option_c (fold c4 into cuda_kernels).
recommendation.rationale: Current empirical evidence suggests threshold is above 6 uses on production paths (40/40 PASS at 6). Adding 1 use with deps fully inside existing graph has the lowest structural risk. Option D in parallel gives concrete threshold data that helps future integrations (C2 layer fused FFI will add a module too).
post_edit_validation_sequence: 3. grep -c '^c4_' /tmp/train_clm_gpu_post.c → non-zero (confirms c4 symbols reached codegen)
6. If all 5 PASS: proceed with F1-TC integration per clm_7b_launch_checklist#f1_tc_integration.enable_sequence
7. If any FAIL: fall back to option_c, repeat 1-5

dependency_risk_on_silent_exit_day_when_.note_per_checklist_92_93: clm_7b_launch_checklist#binary_inputs_from_repo.import_chain_risk explicitly calls out this risk with mitigation: build locally + verify composed.c body > 200KB before gcc step. 1.5B composed.c is 291KB reference. 7B with D=4096 SEQ=4096 expects 450-600KB — a < 250KB composed.c is the silent-exit fingerprint.
dependency_risk_on_silent_exit_day_when_.verification_in_launch_sequence_step_2: codegen_on_pod_a line 196: `ls -la /workspace/train_clm_7b_composed.c  # verify size > 250KB` — this check is already in the launch playbook
dependency_risk_on_silent_exit_day_when_.enhancement_recommended: Add to launch_command_sequence preflight: `grep -c 'c4_' /workspace/train_clm_7b_composed.c` → must be > 10 (c4 symbols present)

session_b_coordination.rationale: This is a nexus-local .hexa integration; no hexa-lang stage0 / codegen changes are required. Sibling sessions on hexa-lang parser/codegen (54d486b, 56b80dd) are orthogonal — they may incidentally REDUCE silent-exit risk by purging O(n²) bugs, but their fixes do not directly target this issue and their landing timing is independent.
session_b_coordination.reason_blocking: F1-TC integration is explicitly on the optimistic launch path (clm_7b_launch_checklist.optimistic_launch_utc 2026-04-17T15:00Z). Without F1-TC the 7B step time is 4500ms → 1200ms (3.75x slower) → launch cost +3.75x.
session_b_coordination.fp32_fallback_verdict: If F1-TC integration is blocked by silent-exit for > 6h, launch 7B fp32 single-rank as a 3B fallback at scale_2_8b — per clm_7b_launch_checklist.critical_path_to_launch[3].blocker=false_if_fp32_fallback_ok

post_edit_required_pass: main() body in codegen C non-empty (> 50 lines)
c4_* symbols present in codegen (grep > 10 hits)
closed_loop_verification_plan.ultimate_gate: 7B launch with USE_TC=1 env fires kernel registration message `[train_clm_gpu] F1-TC bf16 WMMA ENABLED` at step 0; step time drops from 4500ms to 1600ms (fp32) or 1200ms (TC+FSDP); first 100 steps log emits loss monotonic down
